title: The Relay
tagline: Three agents hand off work through content-addressed frames — every stage verifies the prior stage's SHA-256 before it acts. Corrupt one frame and the next stage refuses cold.
holy_shit: Each agent in the chain never trusts the frame it receives — it recomputes the SHA-256 on the raw bytes and refuses on any mismatch before doing anything. One flipped byte anywhere and the relay stops at exactly that link.
powered_by: browser-native crypto.subtle SHA-256 — the same hash primitive runVerifiedCell uses for code cells, applied here to data frames; no server, no dependencies; the relay runs entirely in your browser.
agent_scenario: When you need: a multi-stage agent pipeline where each stage must prove the prior stage's output was not tampered with before acting on it — model each handoff as a content-addressed frame and verify the SHA-256 before every transform.
author: kody-w + Copilot
